food_subgroup: Sandwich with poultry, cured meat, and condiment dressing

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for a chicken-bacon-ranch sandwich. FOODON_00002473 (sandwich food product) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FHR covers sandwiches as a category. No specific chicken-bacon-ranch sub-code exists; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226789007 represents the generic sandwich concept; used as closest parent since no specific chicken-bacon-ranch sandwich code exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard ala carte serving is approximately one 6-inch submarine sandwich weighing around 220 g, comprising a bread roll, grilled chicken, bacon, ranch dressing, and salad vegetables.

nutrient_source.title: Subway Chicken & Bacon Ranch Melt, 6-inch — USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Subway Chicken & Bacon Ranch Melt (6-inch), converted to per-100 g basis. The ala carte item sold in Singapore by Subway and similar operators is compositionally equivalent to the standard 6-inch Chicken Bacon Ranch sub. Serving weight estimated at 220 g based on USDA entry weight for a comparable 6-inch sub. Values scaled accordingly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a chicken-bacon-ranch sandwich. GI estimated at 45 based on the GI of a standard white-bread submarine sandwich (GI ~45–50) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). The bread component dominates glycemic response; protein and fat from chicken, bacon, and ranch dressing attenuate the GI relative to plain white bread (GI ~70). GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 51.7) / 100 ≈ 23.3, rounded to 23.0. Serving carbohydrate: 23.5 g/100 g × 220 g = 51.7 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~560 mg per 100 g; ~1232 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this sandwich due to its very high sodium content (~1232 mg per 6-inch serving), which represents more than half the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; requesting no added salt, reduced-sodium bread, and omitting bacon can meaningfully lower sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~23 per serving) driven by refined wheat bread
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that the refined wheat bread in this sandwich contributes a moderate glycemic load of approximately 23 per serving; choosing a wholegrain or multigrain bread option, reducing portion size, and pairing with non-starchy vegetables can help moderate postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from bacon and ranch dressing, combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the combination of saturated fat (~2.8 g per 100 g, primarily from bacon and ranch dressing) and high sodium (~560 mg per 100 g) warrants caution; limiting frequency of consumption, requesting reduced-fat dressing, and omitting bacon can reduce saturated fat and sodium burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Chicken Bacon Ranch Sandwich (Ala Carte) is a Western-style submarine sandwich consisting of a soft wheat bread roll filled with grilled or roasted chicken breast strips, crispy bacon, ranch dressing, and fresh salad vegetables such as lettuce, tomato, onion, and green pepper. It is sold as an ala carte item (without a drink or side) at Subway outlets and similar sandwich chains operating in Singapore, including at Funan mall. The sandwich is assembled to order, with the bread optionally toasted. The flavour profile is savoury and creamy, with the tangy ranch dressing complementing the smoky bacon and mild chicken. Nutritionally, a 6-inch serving (~220 g) provides approximately 480 kcal, 29.7 g protein, 18 g fat, and 51.7 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is high (~1232 mg per serving), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load is moderate (~23 per serving) due to the refined wheat bread, which is relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The protein content is substantial, supporting satiety. This item is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those avoiding gluten or dairy. It is not certified halal in all outlets due to the presence of bacon.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods for a compositionally equivalent Subway Chicken & Bacon Ranch Melt (6-inch). The Singapore ala carte item is expected to be nutritionally similar but may differ in exact bread weight, dressing quantity, and ingredient sourcing. GI is an analogous estimate based on the GI of white-bread submarine sandwiches from the University of Sydney GI Database. No direct Singapore-specific laboratory analysis is available. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are per 100 g, adapted from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods (Subway Chicken & Bacon Ranch Melt, 6-inch) and scaled to a 220 g serving estimate. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not separately reported in the USDA branded entry used. 3. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not separately reported in the USDA branded entry used. 4. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. 5. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. 6. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. 7. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. 8. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. 9. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. 10. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. 11. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. 12. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. 13. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. 14. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. 15. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. 16. Halal status is not confirmed; bacon is typically pork-derived and Subway Singapore does not serve pork products, but the Funan Mix & Match outlet may differ — halal flag set to false pending confirmation. 17. GI is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 18. The food is classified as Western cuisine sold in Singapore; multilingual names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are provided per Singaporean dish multilingual rules, as this item is sold in Singapore and requires local-language coverage.

Documented all -1 sentinel values with explicit reasons in notes.
Estimated GI at 45 using analogous white-bread submarine sandwich data from University of Sydney GI Database; documented estimation basis.
Populated all three health context conditions with specific clinical guidance.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases are perfectly consistent.
Set ontology match_type values to allowed values only (closest_parent).
